commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=145d43e445de009aaa142a8135b49ef195421e5d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds queue-pressure fields to fallback observations, writes cron model/fallback progress into task run status, threads task run ids through cron execution, adds a runtime proof script, and adds related docs.

PR surface: Source +127, Tests +383, Docs +165, Other +143. Total +818 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the embedded cron fallback path calls `runEmbeddedAgent` again for the fallback candidate, and that call reaches queue enqueues that reject once `gatewayDraining` is true.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer or author follow-up on queue-boundary semantics before an automated repair is safe, because bypassing Gateway drain must preserve session/global lane ordering.

target.title: feat(cron): surface fallback progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Stop model fallback progress from aborting already-active work when Gateway drain begins; new queue enqueues still reject during drain.
- Add queue pressure fields to model fallback observations and surface cron primary/fallback progress into detached task run status.
- Sanitize fallback failure detail before it reaches cron task progress, including success-step detail reused from a previous failed candidate.
- Sanitize persisted cron progress fields for ANSI/OSC/control characters before they reach task status surfaces.
- Replace the undefined docs example with source-backed OpenClaw artifacts (`cron task ledgers` and `command lane snapshots`).
- Rebase onto latest `upstream/main` and mark the inherited migration selection compatibility alias as deprecated so the architecture/deprecated-JSDoc gate passes.
- Thread cron task run ids through manual/isolated execution, add a reproducible runtime proof script, and document the production AI governance checklist.

## Verification

- PASS: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/process/command-queue.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.task-progress.test.ts` (3 shards, 112 tests)
- PASS: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- PASS: `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- PASS: `pnpm check:architecture`
- PASS: `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/migrate/skill-selection-prompt.ts src/agents/model-fallback-observation.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.task-progress.test.ts scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- PASS: `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- PASS: `node scripts/docs-list.js`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Already-active cron/background work can continue its configured model fallback chain after Gateway drain begins, while new command enqueues still reject during drain. Cron-style task progress records primary/fallback model status plus queue active/queued/draining state, with fallback failure detail sanitized before status persistence. Persisted cron progress fields strip ANSI/OSC/control characters before task status display surfaces can read them.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout at `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-87343-rebase`; PR head `145d43e445de009aaa142a8135b49ef195421e5d`; rebased/cherry-picked onto `upstream/main@73752f07f21983c1cf19a8a8cb598d815b5cbe04`; proof used an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` created by `scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`, then targeted Vitest, core-test tsgo, full lint, architecture gate, targeted oxfmt, whitespace checks, and docs-list commands listed above.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the committed proof script:

```text
[proof] stateDir=/var/folders/_x/34p90_ts62zcl8g4bvfkyjsw0000gn/T/openclaw-pr-87343-proof-fDnXJU
[proof] task created runtime=cron runId=pr-87343-cron-proof
[proof] initial progressSummary=model primary=openai/gpt-4.1-mini; queue active=0 queued=0 draining=no
[proof] active queue before fallback={"lane":"main","queuedCount":0,"activeCount":1,"maxConcurrent":1,"draining":false,"generation":0}
[proof] attempt provider=openai model=gpt-4.1-mini
[proof] gateway drain marked while active task is still running
[proof] fallback step={"fallbackStepType":"fallback_step","fallbackStepFromModel":"openai/gpt-4.1-mini","fallbackStepToModel":"anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5","fallbackStepFromFailureReason":"rate_limit","fallbackStepFromFailureDetail":"primary rate limited","fallbackStepChainPosition":1,"fallbackStepFinalOutcome":"next_fallback","fallbackStepQueueActive":1,"fallbackStepQueueQueued":0,"fallbackStepQueueDraining":true}
[proof] recorded progressSummary=model fallback: openai/gpt-4.1-mini -> anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5 reason=rate_limit outcome=next_fallback detail=primary rate limited; queue active=1 queued=0 draining=yes
[proof] attempt provider=anthropic model=claude-haiku-3-5
[proof] fallback step={"fallbackStepType":"fallback_step","fallbackStepFromModel":"openai/gpt-4.1-mini","fallbackStepToModel":"anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5","fallbackStepFromFailureReason":"rate_limit","fallbackStepFromFailureDetail":"primary rate limited","fallbackStepChainPosition":2,"fallbackStepFinalOutcome":"succeeded","fallbackStepQueueActive":1,"fallbackStepQueueQueued":0,"fallbackStepQueueDraining":true}
[proof] recorded progressSummary=model fallback: openai/gpt-4.1-mini -> anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5 reason=rate_limit outcome=succeeded detail=primary rate limited; queue active=1 queued=0 draining=yes
[proof] active task result=anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5:fallback ok
[proof] new enqueue rejected with GatewayDrainingError
[proof] final task progressSummary=model fallback: openai/gpt-4.1-mini -> anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5 reason=rate_limit outcome=succeeded detail=primary rate limited; queue active=1 queued=0 draining=yes
[proof] PASS active fallback continued during drain; new enqueue still rejected
```

- Observed result after fix: The active fallback chain reached `anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5` and returned `fallback ok` after `markGatewayDraining()`. The cron-runtime task row ended with a fallback `progressSummary` containing `queue active=1 queued=0 draining=yes`. A new enqueue during drain was still rejected with `GatewayDrainingError`. Regression coverage confirms raw provider API keys/request ids are not carried into success-step fallback detail and terminal control sequences are stripped before cron progress persistence.
- What was not tested: A live external-provider cron run was not executed; the proof script uses production model fallback, command queue, and detached task runtime APIs with a deterministic local provider callback. Full local `pnpm check:changed` was not run; GitHub CI and ClawSweeper re-review are pending on this pushed head.
target.author: riosbotchen-source

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Already-active cron/background work can continue its configured model fallback chain after Gateway drain begins, while new command enqueues still reject during drain. Cron-style task progress records primary/fallback model status plus queue active/queued/draining state, with fallback failure detail sanitized before status persistence. Persisted cron progress fields strip ANSI/OSC/control characters before task status display surfaces can read them.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout at `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-87343-rebase`; PR head `145d43e445de009aaa142a8135b49ef195421e5d`; rebased/cherry-picked onto `upstream/main@73752f07f21983c1cf19a8a8cb598d815b5cbe04`; proof used an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` created by `scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/cron-fallback-progress-proof.ts`, then targeted Vitest, core-test tsgo, full lint, architecture gate, targeted oxfmt, whitespace checks, and docs-list commands listed above.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the committed proof script:

```text

[proof] recorded progressSummary=model fallback: openai/gpt-4.1-mini -> anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5 reason=rate_limit outcome=next_fallback detail=primary rate limited; queue active=1 queued=0 draining=yes
[proof] attempt provider=anthropic model=claude-haiku-3-5
[proof] fallback step={"fallbackStepType":"fallback_step","fallbackStepFromModel":"openai/gpt-4.1-mini","fallbackStepToModel":"anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5","fallbackStepFromFailureReason":"rate_limit","fallbackStepFromFailureDetail":"primary rate limited","fallbackStepChainPosition":2,"fallbackStepFinalOutcome":"succeeded","fallbackStepQueueActive":1,"fallbackStepQueueQueued":0,"fallbackStepQueueDraining":true}

[proof] PASS active fallback continued during drain; new enqueue still rejected
```

- Observed result after fix: The active fallback chain reached `anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5` and returned `fallback ok` after `markGatewayDraining()`. The cron-runtime task row ended with a fallback `progressSummary` containing `queue active=1 queued=0 draining=yes`. A new enqueue during drain was still rejected with `GatewayDrainingError`. Regression coverage confirms raw provider API keys/request ids are not carried into success-step fallback detail and terminal control sequences are stripped before cron progress persistence.
- What was not tested: A live external-provider cron run was not executed; the proof script uses production model fallback, command queue, and detached task runtime APIs with a deterministic local provider callback. Full local `pnpm check:changed` was not run; GitHub CI and ClawSweeper re-review are pending on this pushed head.
